hypothesis: Enhancing SSTR2 selectivity by introducing aromatic residues at position 5 to better complement SSTR2-specific ECL2/TM5 interactions, and hydrophobic substitutions at position 11 to avoid off-target SSTR3/5 binding.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy produces strong SSTR2 binders with good selectivity, but structural quality remains a bottleneck. Tighter clash constraints and targeted mutation of non-pharmacophore positions will improve structural quality while preserving selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: Low pLDDT and high ddG suggest poor structural quality due to clashes. Tightening clash tolerance will enforce better geometry.

Despite good selectivity, structural failures dominate. Focusing on non-pharmacophore positions known to drive off-target contacts (SSTR3/SSTR5) will allow more precise mutation control to preserve selectivity while improving structure.
